novel_features.name: Quality-blend discovery
novel_features.description: Find models that are both highly rated and printable on your setup, ranked by a composite quality score from your local mirror; constrain with --printable, --no-ams, --material, or --max-weight.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining per-instance rating data against download/print counts in local SQLite — no scraper or the MakerWorld UI exposes a combined quality filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs models filtered by real quality and printer fit, not just popularity.

novel_features.name: Designer-watch deltas
novel_features.description: See which tracked designers posted new models or climbed in rank since your last sync.
novel_features.rationale: Set-diffs each designer's mirrored catalog between two sync snapshots; MakerWorld has no 'new since last visit'.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to answer 'what changed for the creators I follow' without re-scanning every profile.

novel_features.name: Trend movers
novel_features.description: Rank models by the biggest jump in likes, downloads, or prints between your two most recent syncs.
novel_features.rationale: A reproducible time-delta over local snapshots; the platform's server-ranked Trending is opaque and non-reproducible.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this over 'browse --nav Trending' when the question is what is rising right now, not what is popular overall.

novel_features.name: Multi-tag discovery
novel_features.description: List the most common tags across your synced catalog, or find models matching ALL of several tags at once.
novel_features.rationale: A case-insensitive multi-tag intersection over the local mirror — the MakerWorld UI only filters by one tag at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to narrow to models matching a precise combination of tags, not just one.

narrative.headline: Every MakerWorld model, searchable offline — plus trend deltas, designer-watch, and multi-tag discovery no scraper or the web UI offers.
narrative.value_prop: A fast, agent-native CLI over MakerWorld's public catalog. Browse, search, and inspect 3D models from the terminal, mirror them into a local SQLite database for offline full-text search, then run queries the platform never exposes: what is newly rising (movers), which tracked designers shipped (designers deltas), and which models match a precise tag combination (tags). Reads need no account; an optional token unlocks 3MF downloads and your favorites.
narrative.auth_narrative: Browsing, search, and model details work with no credentials against Bambu Lab's public API. To download 3MF files or read your favorites, set MAKERWORLD_TOKEN to a Bambu Cloud token (the same JWT the Bambu Handy app uses).

quickstart.comment: Health check — confirms the CLI and API host are reachable; needs no auth.
See what is trending right now, straight from the public API.
Mirror a slice of the catalog into local SQLite for offline search and deltas.
Surface high-quality models from your local mirror.

troubleshoots.symptom: the live keyword search returns no results for a query
troubleshoots.fix: Some queries are rank-gated; retry with: designs search <kw> --order-by hotScore, or sync then use offline FTS: search <kw>.

troubleshoots.symptom: movers or designers deltas returns empty
troubleshoots.fix: These need at least two syncs to diff. Run 'sync' again later, then re-run.
Set MAKERWORLD_TOKEN to a current Bambu Cloud JWT; tokens expire after ~90 days.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or script needs MakerWorld catalog data as structured JSON: discovering printable models by quality or printer fit, tracking designers and trends over time, or resolving a model to its downloadable 3MF. It shines for repeated, queryable access where the web UI's per-visit browsing and opaque ranking fall short.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to slice models or generate G-code — it fetches catalog data and 3MF files, not slicer output.
Do not use it to control or monitor a physical Bambu printer (MQTT/print jobs) — it is catalog-only.
Do not use it to publish, edit, or moderate your own designs — it is read-and-download oriented.

recipes.title: Inspect a model's printer requirements (deeply nested — narrow with --select)
recipes.explanation: Pull just the fields that decide whether a model fits your printer, instead of the full multi-KB design payload.

recipes.title: Find newly-rising household models
recipes.explanation: Rank models by download growth between your two most recent syncs.

recipes.title: Track what your favorite designers shipped
recipes.explanation: Roll up new uploads and rank rises across all synced designers.

recipes.title: Only models printable without AMS
recipes.explanation: Constrain discovery to single-material models that print on a base machine (no AMS).
